_doc: The enforce-by-absence ledger (ported discipline from live_visuals_4). Each collapse made something PERMANENTLY gone or single-homed; tools/structural_absence_check.py asserts it stays that way, with the rationale next to each entry so a future engineer who resurrects one gets a loud red instead of silent drift.

reference_must_not_exist.why: the self-laundering running_code remedy: it re-blessed whatever branch was running, turning the drift warning green. Replaced in step 0 by the CRITICAL deployed_trunk gate + ops/launch.sh (always returns to the trunk).
the WARN, self-laundering running_code probe is replaced by the CRITICAL deployed_trunk probe (step 0). It must not return.

the trailing-'?' question heuristic. It fabricated a high-severity 'it's asking you' decision out of ANY assistant turn that merely ended in a question mark (incl. a dev assistant's own writing in a watched window), buzzing the user about questions that were never asked. Deleted 2026-06-19; a `question` is now detected ONLY via an explicit AskQuestion/askFollowup tool call (cursor_registry._extract_ask_question).

the 'finished -> invent a next step' auto-proposal. On every watched-thread completion it called Claude to manufacture a 'next step' and pushed it to the phone as a tap-to-approve decision, framing a window the user merely finished driving as something asking for a decision (the live_visuals_4/ucs 'fabricated question' the user hit 2026-06-19). Deleted; a completion is silent-audit-only — only an explicit ask buzzes.

the cheap Claude call behind the deleted auto-proposal (_maybe_propose_next_after_completion). Its only job was to invent a next-step decision to buzz; with the proposal gone it is dead. Removed 2026-06-19 — do not resurrect a 'guess what the user wants next' model call on the cursor-watch path.

single_reader.why: approval posture has ONE home (config). Only the dispatcher (src/mcp.py) and the judge (src/judge.py, via the posture clause) may read it. A new reader is a second home and re-opens the spec/config contradiction step 2 collapsed.

awaiting_collapse.pattern: except Exception: pass  /  except (..., Exception): pass
awaiting_collapse.why: broad-swallow silent failures (ARCHITECTURE Fundamental 3 forbids try/except: pass). Pre-existing debt concentrated in voice/cleanup paths (preflight, bot, gemini_session, discord_voice, cursor_registry, claude_code). The next collapse narrows each catch to the specific exception or lets it raise — done with the live bot available to test the voice/cleanup paths. Tracked here so it is VISIBLE, not silently accepted. Narrow idiomatic catches (CancelledError, queue.Empty/Full, ImportError, FileNotFoundError, TimeoutError) are NOT in scope.
